Kem's account of Lord Aldraste's decline now uses different syntax and moment-specific wording while preserving the twenty-six-year exposure and year-fifteen turning point.

finding_results.notes: After the garrison reveal, the ending now advances through implication, tactical body-count, Sable's southbound decision, her warning about the commander, and her departure instead of repeating the threshold thesis in multiple explanatory paragraphs.
Vel's entrance now uses file-category context plus one new observational gap, without re-listing age, build, scarred hands, graying hair, or prior economy-of-movement phrasing.
Dren is introduced through his bandaged arm, position by the packs, and immediate practical posture rather than the earlier stocky/road-worn/tanned inventory.
Kem's portrait baseline is compressed to a single clause before the present-tense contrast takes over.
The opening now registers the Margins through Sable's exposure-based contrast with the Vault instead of repeating the overworked-soil metaphor from prior chapters.
Whole-chapter check found 0 em-dash lines across 114 prose lines.
Whole-chapter sentence check found 11 'She'-opened sentences out of 256 measured sentences, below the 7% target.

The sequence is now explicit: Sable's last report to Lady Aldraste occurs while the team is still with her, she dismisses the team afterward, then files the false mission-complete report and continues alone.

Three changes made to break thesis-speech pattern in the post-Red-Reserve-reveal section. (1) Vel's balanced aphorism ('Untrained three-hundreds break whatever they hit. Trained two-fifties choose where it breaks.') replaced with a concrete tactical assessment referencing Sable's team's failure and ending with a glance at Dren's injured arm — threat is specific and immediate, not quotable. (2) Kem's clean system diagnosis ('So zero wasn't what scared them...') replaced with a pressured, self-correcting reaction that starts from his father's deterioration, breaks mid-sentence, and restarts quieter with a partial understanding ('They knew what was down there') rather than a finished political analysis. (3) Sable's thematic farewell ('what the last twenty-seven years were helping to keep shut') replaced with concrete items she needs to verify ('the threshold. The garrison.') and a personal coda ('Not from a file.'); commander warning compressed from a quotable flourish to blunt operational shorthand. Red Reserve threat, Sable's southbound choice, and all operational facts preserved. Kem's reaction includes explicit self-correction per acceptance test requirement.

pass_summaries.summary: Revised the chapter around a cleaner Rennic proof scene, removed repeated cross-chapter re-introductions, and rebuilt the post-garrison ending so each paragraph adds consequence or choice instead of re-certifying the chapter thesis. The draft also clears the measurable craft targets with 0 em-dash lines and 11 'She'-opened sentences out of 256 measured sentences.

Single finding addressed with three targeted dialogue edits in the Red Reserve reveal sequence (lines 191-221). Vel's combat assessment, Kem's reaction to the garrison truth, and Sable's departure speech were all roughened from polished thesis-speech into character-specific pressured responses: Vel answers tactically with concrete stakes, Kem processes through his father's deterioration with a mid-sentence break, and Sable commits to going south without explaining the chapter's thematic significance to the room. All operational content, setups (sable_question, threshold_garrison), and the chapter's must-land beats preserved.

Revised the packeted prose-engine repeats in Chapter 15, varying the cited sentence shapes and tightening adjacent copyedit issues without changing the chapter's state shift, continuity, or voice profile.
